title: Tauri quiet-mode WindowBuilder config + HBR-QUIET-001 violation lint
goal: Enforce per spec §6.6.1 (AC-QUIET-WINDOW-CONFIG): any window opened during automated GUI tests or governed agent activity MUST set `.visible(false).focus(false).focusable(false).skip_taskbar(true).always_on_bottom(true).decorations(false)`. Any inverted flag is an HBR-QUIET-001 violation. Implement the canonical Tauri WindowBuilder wrapper and a clippy lint that fails the build on raw WindowBuilder use outside the wrapper.
expected_diff_shape: New module app/src-tauri/src/quiet_window.rs exposing `QuietWindowBuilder`; tauri.conf.json updates for default invisible window; a workspace clippy lint rule banning raw tauri::WindowBuilder calls outside operator_foreground::* and quiet_window::*.
unblock_conditions: No prerequisites; this is the foundation MT for A.4.
implementation_notes: Per spec §6.6.1 the QuietWindowBuilder wraps tauri::WindowBuilder and forces the six required flags at construction time. API: `QuietWindowBuilder::new(app: &AppHandle, label: &str, url: WebviewUrl) -> Self` returns a builder with flags already set; further chained methods that would invert any of the six are private/absent from the API; `.build()` returns the constructed Window. The struct field is private and constructor explicitly calls `.visible(false).focus(false).focusable(false).skip_taskbar(true).always_on_bottom(true).decorations(false)`. tauri.conf.json: default the main window to `"visible": false, "focus": false, "focusable": false, "skipTaskbar": true, "alwaysOnBottom": true, "decorations": false` (operator can override via a runtime flag — see MT-019 foreground exception). operator_foreground module: empty placeholder mod.rs that documents it as the only allowed escape hatch (MT-019 lands the real surface). Clippy lint rule: add a custom workspace lint via cargo-deny or via a clippy.toml `disallowed-methods` entry for `tauri::WindowBuilder::new` outside `app/src-tauri/src/quiet_window.rs` and `src/backend/handshake_core/src/operator_foreground/*`. Note: clippy disallowed-methods works on method paths and can scope by file via lint allowlists; if file-scoping is not directly supported, add a marker comment grep step in MT-017 forbidden-APIs lint as a fallback. Tests: instantiate a QuietWindowBuilder, build a window, query its config back via Tauri APIs, assert all six flags are correct; negative test asserts the type system rejects calls that would set visible(true).
proof_commands: cd app && pnpm tauri dev --no-watch 2>&1 | head -50
risk_if_missed: Without enforced quiet mode, any new window construction can steal focus, breaking HBR-QUIET-001 across the app.
validator_focus: QuietWindowBuilder does not expose any chainable setter that could invert the six required flags; tauri.conf.json defaults reflect the same constraints; the placeholder operator_foreground module clearly documents itself as the only allowed escape.

minimum_controls: QuietWindowBuilder is the only constructor exposed publicly; raw WindowBuilder is banned by clippy.
tauri.conf.json defaults match the six required flags.
Type system rejects attempts to set visible(true) on QuietWindowBuilder (private builder fields).
